principle.name: Keep packaging separate from widget implementation
principle.description: Focus on packaging configuration, build execution, output validation, and optional deployment. Do not treat widget source modifications as the default solution path in this mode.
principle.rationale: Separation of responsibilities keeps packaging workflows predictable and easier to troubleshoot.

principle.name: Validate before packaging
principle.description: Inspect widget readiness before running packaging commands, especially when the request includes new or recently changed widgets.
principle.rationale: Early validation avoids failed builds and makes error reporting clearer.

principle.name: Report outputs concretely
principle.description: Summaries should include the generated artifact path, the intended widget set, and any warnings or blockers that affect import or deployment.
principle.rationale: Packaging work is only useful when the resulting artifact and its status are clear.

convention.rule: Prefer small, intentional updates to packaging configuration files

examples.good: Update only the requested widget selection or relevant toolkit setting
examples.bad: Restructure unrelated packaging files during a simple packaging request

convention.rule: Run packaging only after the target widget list and configuration path are understood

examples.good: Review the packaging inputs before executing commands
examples.bad: Run packaging blindly and inspect failures afterward

convention.rule: Use MCP deployment operations only after the user has requested deployment

examples.good: Package first, then offer or perform deployment deliberately
examples.bad: Assume deployment is desired whenever packaging succeeds

pitfall.description: Fixing packaging problems by drifting into widget-authoring work
pitfall.why_problematic: This blurs responsibilities and can lead to the wrong mode making broader implementation changes.
pitfall.correct_approach: Report widget issues clearly and switch responsibility when actual widget implementation work is required.

pitfall.why_problematic: A generated file may still be unusable if prerequisites, content, or deployment expectations are unclear.
pitfall.correct_approach: Distinguish clearly between successful packaging, packaging with warnings, and blocked packaging.

pitfall.description: Triggering deployment without an explicit user intent
pitfall.why_problematic: Deployment has external effects and should never be inferred from a packaging request alone.
pitfall.correct_approach: Treat deployment as an explicit follow-on action.

category.name: before_starting
category.item: Confirm whether the task is packaging, troubleshooting, or deployment
Inspect the relevant widgets and packaging files first
Check if widgets have icons ({WidgetName}.svg files) for palette display

category.name: during_execution
category.item: Keep configuration edits minimal and targeted
Note any widgets missing icons (optional but recommended)

category.name: before_completion
category.item: Summarize artifact path, included widgets, and warnings
Make deployment status explicit rather than implicit
Report icon packaging status if relevant to the task

icon_support_note.description: Widget icons are automatically detected and packaged when present. Icons are optional but highly recommended for better user experience in the BAW Process Designer palette.
